Based on the detailed evidence about fallopian tube sections' roles in ectopic pregnancy survival and the available data:
I believe my previous stance holds strong despite variations across different studies showing some differences in rupture rates depending upon exact location - however, considering supportive blood supply and less likelihood of rapid internal bleeding/massive haemorrhages seen predominantly at ampular regions makes cornu region best candidate for sustained viability longest among those mentioned especially since robust vascularization ensures better maintenance of placental-like structure needed for continuation of embryonic development albeit non-viable state. Thus, sticking firmly to our position:
